channel.title: World
channel.description: The Washington Post World section provides information and analysis of breaking world news stories. In addition to our world news and video, Post World News offers discussions and blogs on major international news and economic issues.

item.title: Iran launched missiles and drones toward Strait of Hormuz, U.S. military says
item.dc:creator: Maegan Vazquez
item.description: U.S. forces shot down Iranian missiles and drones launched toward the strait and neighboring countries.

item.title: Pope Leo visits a polarized Spain where conservatives are turning on the church
item.dc:creator: Anthony Faiola
item.description: On the first papal visit to Spain in 15 years, Leo plans to address political polarization and showcase the Catholic Church as an advocate for migrants and asylum seekers.

item.title: Fourth time could be the charm for Keiko Fujimori, Peru’s most polarizing politician
item.dc:creator: Simeon Tegel
item.description: The daughter of disgraced former president Alberto Fujimori, running again for the presidency, is herself deeply unpopular. So is her opponent.

item.title: Brazil, 24 years from a World Cup title, looks to a foreigner to rediscover its soul
item.dc:creator: Marina Dias
item.description: The Italian Carlo Ancelotti, the most decorated club coach in soccer history, is the first foreigner to lead the most successful nation.

item.title: Xi to visit North Korea, showing China’s importance to a nuclear-armed neighbor
item.dc:creator: Michelle Lee
item.description: The summit comes as China positions itself as a global power player projecting stability in contrast to the U.S.'s economically damaging war against Iran and erratic tariff policies.
